channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Big Tech Execs Take on Key Roles in Trump Administration
item.description: President Trump has appointed a number of tech executives to key roles in his second administration, including Elon Musk and David Sacks.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Microsoft joins the European cloud group that sued it for antitrust
item.description: Microsoft seems to be a believer in the adage that if you can’t beat them, join them. Shortly after Microsoft executives settled antitrust claims with CISPE, a major European cloud organization, last summer, they began negotiating with the group to become a member.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Extreme cold and snow across the South isn't a threat to most native plants and animals, experts say
item.description: A deep freeze and rare snow have brought most things to a standstill from parts of Texas to Florida, but experts say wildlife natives like cypress trees and prairie plants, and cold-blooded gators and snakes, should be alright.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sustainability, grid demands, AI workloads will challenge data center growth in 2025
item.description: Data centers this year will face several challenges as the demand for artificial intelligence introduces an evolution in AI hardware, on-premises and cloud-based strategies for training and inference, and innovations in power distributions—all while opposition to new data center developments continu
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Red Hat unveils Kubernetes connectivity solution to tame multi-cloud chaos
item.description: Integrating traffic management, policy enforcement, and role-based access control, Red Hat Connectivity Link is a new technology from IBM’s Red Hat business unit that’s aimed at simplifying how enterprises manage application connectivity across distributed cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ensuring SMBs don’t get left behind in the Gen AI wave
item.description: Don't let your SMB get left behind! Harness AI's power with expert guidance and the right tools for transformative growth.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cloud repatriation – how to balance repatriation effectively and securely
item.description: Public cloud is set to increase in popularity, however some are moving to private cloud. Here are the pros and cons of both.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple's Mail App to Get AI-Powered Email Summary and Prioritization
item.description: Apple is set to introduce AI-powered email summary and prioritization systems on iPads and Macs, mirroring the features available on iPhones.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A guide to integrating application security into any cyber defense strategy
item.description: Integrating application security into a broader security framework is critical as applications are often the entry points for attackers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: En marcha QDQ-GEO: el primer sistema geoestacionario de distribución de clave cuántica por satélite
item.description: Un paso adelante en materia de seguridad cuántica e innovación tecnológica para el sector aeroespacial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The iPhone SE 4 could surprisingly get this iPhone 16 feature –and that would make it an even better mid-range option
item.description: The rumors suggest the iPhone SE 4 will have a Dynamic Island like the iPhone 16, as well as matching its internal components.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The bitter lesson for generative AI adoption
item.description: The rapid development and improvement in generative AI technology pose a challenge for training and fine-tuning as a sustainable path for adoption. If organizations constantly need to fine-tune new models for specific tasks, they might be in a costly cycle of catching up with new technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump vexes New Zealanders by claiming one of their proudest historical moments for America
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's declaration in an inauguration speech that Americans “split the atom” prompted vexed social media posts on Tuesday by New Zealanders, who said the achievement belonged to a pioneering scientist revered in his homeland.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk's prominence during inauguration ceremonies stamps tech titan's place in Trump's orbit
item.description: The blossoming relationship between President Donald Trump and tech titan Elon Musk was on full display throughout Monday's inauguration ceremonies.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also are among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Army testing approach to ease communication with allies
item.description: Col. Heath Giesecke, the director of the Army’s Enterprise Cloud Management Agency, said a cloud-based commercial app is helping close a communication gap. The post Army testing approach to ease communication with allies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Jeffries: Trump expected to find way for TikTok sale to US owner
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said President-elect Trump is expected to find a way for TikTok’s sale to a U.S. owner.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Chinese electric hypercar jumps over spikes, potholes while driving itself
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the $236,000 Yangwang U9 electric hypercar boasts 1,287 horsepower and can jump obstacles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to stop robocalls on your flip phone
item.description: Flip phones are harder to block spam calls from, but it's not impossible. Kurt the "CyberGuy" explains how to stop incoming robocalls on your flip phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: That Apple ID disabled message? It's a dangerous scam
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy breaks down why those emails that warn that "Your Apple ID has been disabled" is most likely a scam. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do
item.description: Robin Carnahan, the out-going GSA administrator, said the federal footprint shrunk by 11 million square feet and saved $2 billion in 2024 alone. The post Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Best ways to give your old iPhone a second life
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the many different ways you can repurpose that old iPhone of yours rather than trashing it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Support for Microsoft 365 on Windows 10 ends in mid-October
item.description:
Support for Microsoft 365 ends along with Windows 10 PixieMe/Shutterstock
Although Microsoft announced some time ago that Windows 10 will only be supported (free of charge) until October 14, 2025, the switch to its successor Windows 11 is only taking place slowly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Easterly hails CISA’s ‘world-class’ talent in final address
item.description: CISA grew rapidly during the Biden administration, recruiting technical experts to lead key initiatives like "secure by design" and cyberthreat hunting teams. The post Easterly hails CISA’s ‘world-class’ talent in final address first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
